answer:
Several providers offer self-serve YouTube transcript APIs with free tiers, though their models vary.

YoutrackTranscript.dev [1][2] and DownloadYouTubeTranscripts.com [3] use credit-based systems. YoutrackTranscript.dev provides 10 free credits monthly, requires an API key, and offers rate limits of 1 request per second on the free plan [2]. DownloadYouTubeTranscripts.com also includes 10 free credits upon sign-up with no credit card required [3]. 

YouTube-Transcript.ai [4] offers a keyless, zero-setup service with fair-use limits. You can make GET requests directly to their endpoint without an API key or SDK [4]:

```bash
curl https://youtube-transcript.ai/transcript/{VIDEO_ID}.txt
```

Supadata [5] provides a developer-focused API with a free tier of 100 requests, requiring an API key for access:

```bash
curl 'https://api.supadata.ai/v1/transcript?url={VIDEO_URL}' \
  -H 'x-api-key: {YOUR_API_KEY}'
```

Each provider includes documentation for integrating these endpoints into your applications [1][3][5][4].
